_meta.description: Cross-check baselines for the RapidMetaPrediction engine's single-axis pool. Three cases: (1) homogeneous closed-form (τ²=0 by construction; pooled mean and SE are the exact inverse-variance values); (2) hand-verifiable heterogeneous case where μ_FE, Q_FE, and DL-τ² can be confirmed by paper-and-pencil and the PM-REML estimate is frozen from RapidMetaPrediction v1.0.0; (3) the 6-cohort KFRE real-data fixture, frozen from RapidMetaPrediction v1.0.0 — these are the values that should be bit-reproduced by metafor::rma(yi, vi, method='PM', test='knha') on identical inputs.
_meta.planned_r_crosscheck: v1.1 build-time WebR (or local Rscript) invocation of metafor::rma(yi, vi, method='PM', test='knha') and metamisc::valmeta(...) with the same logit-C inputs, with |Δ| < 1e-4 acceptance on each frozen value. Engine pooling primitives (Paule-Mandel iteration; HKSJ floor max(1, Q/(k-1)); Student-t_{k-1}) are exactly the primitives metafor uses.
primitive_references: Paule RC, Mandel J. J Res Natl Bur Stand 1982;87(5):377-385 — Paule-Mandel τ² iteration
Hartung J, Knapp G. Stat Med 2001;20(24):3875-3889 — HKSJ small-sample CI
Cochrane Handbook v6.5 §10.10.4.3 (Nov 2024) — PI t_{k-1} convention; HKSJ floor max(1, Q/(k-1))
Viechtbauer W. J Stat Softw 2010;36(3):1-48 — metafor::rma defaults and PM implementation

case_1_homogeneous_closed_form.rationale: All y_i equal → μ = 1 by symmetry; SE_μ = sqrt(1 / Σ(1/v_i)) = sqrt(0.04/3). Q = Σ w_i (y_i - μ)² = 0. PM iteration converges to τ²=0 since Q < k-1. HKSJ floor = max(1, 0/(k-1)) = 1. CI = μ ± t_{0.975, k-1} × SE_μ × √(hksj_factor). t_{0.975, 2} = 4.30265… (verified vs Abramowitz & Stegun).

hand_verifiable.rationale: All v_i equal → FE pooled mean = arithmetic mean = 1.0. Q_FE = (1/v) × Σ(y_i - 1)² = 100 × (0.25 + 0 + 0.25) = 50. DL τ² = (Q - df) / (Σw - Σw²/Σw) = (50 - 2) / (300 - 30000/300) = 48 / 200 = 0.24. PM τ² differs from DL because PM solves a different moment equation; PM is frozen below from engine output.

expected_pm_reml.note_pm_equals_dl_here: PM converges to the same τ² = 0.24 as DerSimonian-Laird because for symmetric balanced equal-variance data the PM moment equation Σw_i(y_i-μ_w)² = k-1 has the same root as DL's. Hand check: w = 1/(0.01+τ²), Σw(y-1)² = 2w·0.5 = w. Setting equal to k-1=2 gives w=2... wait, that gives τ² < 0. Re-derive: the spread (y-μ_w)² sums to 0.25+0+0.25 = 0.5, so Σw(y-μ_w)² = 0.5w. Setting 0.5w = 2 gives w = 4, so τ² = 0.25 - 0.01 = 0.24.

case_3_kfre_real_data_6_cohort.input_summary: 6 published KFRE external-validation cohorts (Major 2019, Lennartz 2016, Tangri 2020, Ali 2021, Gallego-Valcarce 2024, Irish 2023). C-statistic logit-scale yi/vi derived from published 95% CIs via delta method.
case_3_kfre_real_data_6_cohort.rationale: Frozen from RapidMetaPrediction v1.0.0 PM-REML + HKSJ. The 0.875 pooled C is consistent with the Tangri 2016 multinational IPD-MA pooled 5-year C of 0.88 (95% CI 0.86–0.90) [PMID 26757465] despite the IPD-MA using a different (Reitsma-flavoured) pooling. The HKSJ-inflated CI of [0.024, 0.999] correctly surfaces the extreme between-cohort heterogeneity (C range 0.73–0.96 across primary-care, advanced-CKD, transplant, and population-linkage settings) — this is honest behaviour, not engine pathology. The 95% prediction interval [0.41, 0.99] reflects what a 7th external validation cohort would be expected to produce.
case_3_kfre_real_data_6_cohort.probast_per_cohort_rationale: Major 2019 (large multi-practice EHR), Tangri 2020 (4-cohort transplant), Irish 2023 (population linkage) → all-low domains, overall low. Lennartz 2016 (small referral centre) → participants+analysis unclear → overall unclear. Ali 2021 (advanced-CKD aetiology subgroups, small per-subgroup N) and Gallego-Valcarce 2024 (single-centre N=339, events-per-variable concern) → participants/analysis high → overall high.
